title: Secure Shell
description: Encrypted protocol for secure remote login, command execution, and file transfer.
fullExplanation: SSH replaced Telnet and rsh which sent everything in plaintext. SSH provides encrypted terminal sessions, secure file transfer (SFTP/SCP), and port forwarding (tunneling). The SSH protocol has three layers: Transport Layer (encryption, integrity, compression), User Authentication Layer (password, public key, keyboard-interactive), and Connection Layer (multiplexes multiple channels over one connection).

authMethods.description: Username + password. Vulnerable to brute force. Disable if possible.
RSA/ECDSA/Ed25519 key pair. Private key stays on client. Most secure.
SSH CA signs user/host certificates. Scales better than key pairs.

keyFacts: SSH-1 is broken — always use SSH-2
Ed25519 keys: faster and more secure than RSA-2048
Disable password auth: PasswordAuthentication no in sshd_config
Fail2ban: auto-ban IPs after failed login attempts
SSH agent forwarding: use local keys on remote hosts (risk: agent hijacking)
Known_hosts file: prevents MITM by storing host fingerprints
Port 22 is heavily scanned — consider changing to non-standard port
